rulings.Case Title: STANLEY PUCHOSIC VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 21, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (premises owner/contractor liability) and denied as to issue 2 (punitive damages). As to issue 1, as pled, plaintiff¿s cause of action for premises owner/contractor liability, as it relates to contractors, does not require a showing that defendant owned, operated, maintained or controlled the premises. While this likely means that there is no substantive difference between plaintiffs¿ causes of action for negligence and premises owner/contractor liability, that is not a basis to grant summary adjudication and is more appropriately handled by the trial judge through jury instructions or otherwise. As to issue 2, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CANDICE CLARK, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Claim From Plaintiffs¿ Complaint For Wrongful Death And Survival
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 21, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant John Crane Inc.¿s motion to strike is ruled upon as follows: Defendant¿s motion as it relates to plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is off calendar as moot given the ruling on defendant¿s demurrer. Defendant¿s motion is denied as to paragraph 2 of the complaint and granted as to paragraph 3 of the complaint. Paragraph 3 is irrelevant. Defendant¿s motion is denied as to the prayer for damages for loss of earning capacity. The complaint alleges that Candice Clark is the successor in interest to William Coburn, deceased. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CANDICE CLARK, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 21, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant John Crane Inc.¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
